original: RECITALS A. We are in the business of franchising others to operate “Pizza Fusion” restaurant businesses, which feature a competitively priced menu of organic and all-natural gourmet pizza and related food specialties, and standards and methods of operation designed to be “green,” and which are operated in buildings that bear our interior and/or exterior trade dress (each a “ Restaurant ” or “ Franchised Business ”).
ollama_generated: RECITALS B. Our company engages in franchising third parties to manage restaurants under the "Pizza Fusion" brand, offering a menu of affordably priced organic and natural gourmet pizzas, alongside unique food specialties, and adhering to environmentally-friendly standards and operational methods. These establishments are distinguished by our trademark interior and/or exterior designs (each referred to as a " Franchised Establishment" or "Licensed Business"). The emphasis here is on the franchisee's responsibility to uphold these unique aspects of the brand.

original: D. You understand the importance of our high standards of quality, appearance, and service and the necessity of operating your Franchised Business in accordance with this Agreement and our standards, specifications and procedures.
ollama_generated:
Clause D:
It is acknowledged that adherence to our exacting standards for quality, presentation, and service is essential for the successful operation of your Franchise Business. As such, you are expected to manage your franchise in harmony with this Agreement, as well as our guidelines, specifications, and procedures. Any deviation may potentially impact the reputation we have collectively built.

original: Page 1 of 39 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 1. RIGHTS GRANTED 1.1. Grant of Franchise.
modified:
Clause:
Page 1 of 39 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015
1. RIGHTS ASCERTAINED
1.1. Assumption of Franchisee Rights.

In this revised clause, the focus shifts from the grantor (PF Hospitality Group Inc.) to the grantee (the Franchisee). The Franchisee is now assuming their rights rather than being granted them by the hospitality group. This alteration subtly implies a greater level of responsibility and initiative on the part of the Franchisee, although it maintains the professional and fluent tone required in legal documentation.

original: 1.2.2 You may advertise and market the Franchised Business and directly solicit customers only within your Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area (subject to Section 9.3  [Regional Fund]  below). You agree not to: (a) advertise or market the services of your Franchised Business outside of the Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area; and/or (b) engage in direct solicitation of customers outside of the Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area. The term “ direct solicitation ” includes, but is not limited to, solicitation in person, by telephone, by mail, by e-mail, the internet, or other electronic means, advertising, marketing, and by distribution of brochures, business cards or other materials.
ollama_generated:
1.2.2 The Franchisee is granted permission to promote and sell the Franchised Business, as well as directly approach potential customers, exclusively within their designated Delivery/Catering and Advertising Territory (subject to Section 9.3 regarding the Regional Fund). The Franchisee undertakes to avoid:

(a) Promoting or advertising the services of the Franchised Business beyond the boundaries of the Delivery/Catering and Advertising Territory; and
(b) Initiating direct contact with customers outside of the defined territory.

For the purpose of this agreement, "direct contact" encompasses but is not limited to face-to-face interactions, phone calls, written correspondence (including emails), internet-based communication, marketing efforts, distributed promotional materials such as brochures, business cards, or other related items.

original: Page 2 of 39 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 1.3. Our Limitations and Our Reserved Rights.  The rights granted to you under this Agreement are not exclusive. We and our affiliates have the right to conduct any business activities, under any name, and at (or from) any location notwithstanding those business activities’ actual or threatened impact on sales of the Franchised Business. For example, we may, among other things, on any terms and conditions we deem advisable, and without granting you any rights therein, do any or all of the following:
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Page 2 of 39 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015
1.3. Our Flexibility and Our Retained Discretion.
The privileges extended to you under this Agreement are non-exclusive. We and our affiliates maintain the discretionary power to engage in any business activities, under any name, and from any location, irrespective of whether such activities have a potential or actual effect on the sales of the Franchised Business. For instance, we reserve the right, among other things, on any terms and conditions deemed suitable by us, to undertake any or all of the following without conferring any rights upon you:

* Operate businesses under our own brand or any other name
* Establish locations that may compete with your Franchised Business
* Collaborate with third parties for mutual benefit, including but not limited to joint ventures, strategic partnerships, and licensing agreements.

original: 1.3.2 We have the right to establish, and license others to establish, businesses under other systems or other proprietary marks, which businesses may offer or sell products and services that are different from the principal products and services offered from the Franchised Business, and which businesses may be located within or outside the Premises, notwithstanding such business’ proximity to the Franchised Business or the Premises or their actual or threatened impact on sales of the Franchised Business.
ollama_generated: 1.3.2 Granted that we retain the liberty to initiate and authorize third parties to initiate, businesses under distinct systems or proprietary trademarks. These independent businesses may market and sell goods and services that deviate from the primary offerings of the Franchisee's Business, irrespective of their location - whether within or outside the Premises. It is acknowledged that such businesses, regardless of their proximity to the Franchisee's Business or the Premises, may potentially influence or affect sales of the Franchisee's Business, yet we assume no responsibility for any negative impact on the sales of the Franchisee's Business arising from these independent businesses.

original: 1.3.3 We have the right to acquire and operate any business of any kind or be acquired by another business, notwithstanding such business’ actual or threatened impact on sales of the Franchised Business.
ollama_generated: 1.3.3 The Franchisee acknowledges and consents that we retain the discretionary right to acquire, establish, or operate any business of any nature, which may or may not have an effect on the sales of the Franchised Business, without being held liable for such actions.

original: The term “ Institutional Facility ” is agreed to mean any outlet that primarily serves the customers located within the facility, such as captive audience facilities and limited purpose or limited access facilities, and includes among other things: airports; train or bus stations; travel plazas; factories; federal, state or local government facilities (including military bases); hospitals and other health-care facilities; stadiums and arenas; recreational facilities; schools, colleges and other academic facilities; convention centers; seasonal facilities; shopping malls; theaters; museums; and workplace cafeterias.
ollama_generated: The term "Institutional Establishment" shall encompass any venue primarily catering to an audience confined within its premises, such as captive audience establishments, limited purpose facilities, or those with restricted access, and may comprise, but are not limited to, the following: airports; train or bus stations; travel plazas; factories; governmental institutions (federal, state, or local, including military bases) hospitals and health-care centers; stadiums and arenas; recreational areas; schools, colleges, and academic institutions; convention centers; seasonal venues; shopping centers; theaters; museums; and workplace canteens. However, it's important to note that this definition does not extend to include establishments where the customers are not primarily confined within the facility's premises.

original: 1.3.6 We have the right to provide, and license others to provide, products or services to Major Accounts (as that term is defined below) at any location, notwithstanding such Major Accounts’ proximity to the Franchised Business or the Premises or their actual or threatened impact on sales at the Franchised Business. The term “ Major Account ” is agreed to mean any customer we designate as such, based upon our sole determination that, because such customer conducts its business at multiple locations and we deem this customer to be of strategic importance, the account, services and pricing of such customer shall be negotiated and secured either (i) by us or (ii) with our assistance, approval and oversight; as further described in Section 6.12  [Major Accounts]  below.
ollama_generated: 1.3.6 It is expressly acknowledged that we retain the discretion to supply and authorize third parties to deliver goods or services to designated Major Accounts (as defined herein), regardless of their geographical proximity to the Franchised Business, the Premises, or any potential influence on sales at the Franchised Business. For the purpose of this agreement, "Major Account" refers to any client whom we deem significant based on our sole judgment, due to their operation across multiple locations and strategic relevance. The account, services, and pricing for such Major Accounts will be negotiated and secured either (i) independently by us or (ii) with our consent and monitoring, as detailed in Section 6.12 [Major Accounts] below. In this context, we assume no obligation to prioritize sales from the Franchised Business over those of any Major Account.

original: 1.4. Limitations.  You agree  not to engage in any of the sales activities that we have reserved to ourselves in Sections 1.3  [Our Limitations and Our Reserved Rights]  above.
modified:
1.4. Expanded Authorizations. We grant you the right to pursue sales activities, excluding those specifically reserved by us as per Sections 1.3 [Our Limitations and Our Reserved Rights], with our explicit, prior written approval. This provision allows for a more expansive approach to your sales activities while maintaining our control over reserved sales activities.

This rewritten clause shifts the responsibility from the client (you) agreeing not to engage in specific sales activities, to us granting permission for these activities. The language has been modified to create a more open and collaborative atmosphere between parties.

original: 2.2.2. You and all Owners must execute the standard form of Pizza Fusion Franchise Agreement that we are then offering to new franchisees (or the standard form that we most recently offered to new franchisees, if we are not at that time actively offering new franchises) (the “Successor Franchise Agreement ”). The terms of the Successor Franchise Agreement may be substantially different from the terms of this Agreement and may require the payment of different fees.
ollama_generated:
2.2.2. We reserve the right to present a revised version of the Pizza Fusion Franchise Agreement, which may supersede this current agreement. The new terms may significantly vary from the existing ones and might involve distinct fee payments. It is your and all Owners' obligation to review and execute such revisions upon our presentation.

This revision ensures that you are aware of our ability to modify the agreement, shifting the responsibility for reviewing and executing any changes onto you, while maintaining a professional tone and clarity in the clause.

original: 2.2.3. You must pay all amounts owed to us, to our affiliates, and to your major suppliers; you must not be in default of this Agreement or any other agreement with us, our affiliates, or our suppliers; and you must have substantially and timely complied with all of your obligations throughout the term of each such agreement.
ollama_generated: 2.2.3. Our right to claim payment from you extends to amounts owed to us, our affiliates, and significant vendors. Your obligation is not to be in breach of this Agreement or any other agreements with us, our affiliates, or our suppliers. However, any failure on your part to meet your obligations will need to be remedied promptly upon notice from us. Compliance with your obligations under each such agreement should be timely and substantially met throughout its duration.

original: Page 4 of 39 Source: PF HOSPITALITY GROUP INC., 10-12G, 9/23/2015 3.2.2. You must calculate and pay the Royalty weekly, based on your Gross Revenues for the previous Period. For purposes of this Agreement, unless otherwise designated by us in writing, a “Period” begins on Monday and ends on the following Sunday.
modified:
Clause:

3.2.2. We reserve the right to request that you calculate and remit the Royalty on a weekly basis, determined by your Gross Revenues for the current Period. For the sake of clarity within this Agreement, unless otherwise specified in writing by us, a “Period” shall commence on Sunday and conclude on the following Saturday.

(In this revised clause, the obligation to calculate and pay royalties is shifted from the obligor to the obligee, and the period begins at the end of the week instead of the beginning.)

original: 3.5. Due Date for Payment . Your Royalty payments and Advertising Contributions are due by the first (1st) day after the end of each Period (or the next business day if the first day is a Sunday or federal holiday) (as of the Agreement Date, the due date is Monday of each week).
modified: 3.5. Payment Timeline: The deadline for your Royalty payments and Advertising Contributions falls on the first (1st) business day following the conclusion of each Period. In case the first day is a Sunday or federal holiday, payment will be due on the subsequent business day. Notably, prior to this revision, payments were originally scheduled for Mondays of each week (as per the Agreement Date).

original: 3.10 Obligations Absolute.  You agree that your obligations to pay us (as well as our affiliates) under this Agreement or any other agreement in connection to the Franchised Business are absolute and unconditional, and not subject to abatement or setoff for past or future claims that you may assert.
modified:
3.10 Unwaivable Obligations. We affirm that your financial obligations towards us (and our affiliates) under this Agreement or any related agreements concerning the Franchised Business are unwaivable and non-negotiable, and shall not be reduced or suspended, regardless of any past or future claims you may assert against us.

In this revised version, the emphasis has been shifted to emphasize that your obligations towards them are unwaivable, while their obligations towards you (potential reductions or suspensions) are no longer absolute or unconditional.

original: (c) We will provide, at no charge, our standard image specifications for the construction of the Franchised Business, improvement of the premises, and for the layout of fixtures, furnishings, equipment, and signs. original: 5. TRAINING 5.1. Initial Training Program 5.1.1. Before you begin operating, the two persons who you designate (and who we find acceptable) to provide managerial responsibilities (each a “ Manager Trainee ”) must all successfully attend (at the same time) and successfully complete our initial training program (we may designate portions of the training program that each person must attend and successfully complete), which is held at our headquarters and/or another location(s) that we specify. One Manager Trainee must be your Operating Principal (who meets the criteria in Section 6.15 of this Agreement), unless we mutually agree otherwise. You may designate another person who will be active in the day-to-day activities and management of the Franchised Business to be the second Manager Trainee. You may ask that additional employees be allowed to attend the portions of the initial training that are designed for your employees, and we will have the right to approve or disapprove that request. All trainees must be persons that we find acceptable at all times to serve in their respective capacities. As of the Effective Date, our training program has two components, as follows:
ollama_generated:
5. TRAINING
5.1 Initial Training Requirements
5.1.1 Each individual designated by you (and deemed acceptable by us) to undertake managerial duties (each referred to hereafter as a " Potential Manager") shall, prior to commencing operations, complete our initial training program. This program may be held at our headquarters or any specified location(s), and the participant(s) will be required to attend and successfully complete all mandatory sessions. However, it is noted that one Potential Manager should meet the criteria outlined in Section 6.15 of this Agreement, but exceptions may be made upon mutual agreement. The second Potential Manager may be any individual you designate to actively participate in the day-to-day operations and management of the Franchised Business.

You have the prerogative to request additional employees' attendance at certain segments of the initial training program designed for your staff. Such requests will be subject to our approval, which may either be granted or denied at our discretion. It is mandatory that all Potential Managers are deemed acceptable by us throughout their tenure in these roles.

As of the Effective Date, our training program consists of two primary components:

a) The initial training program mandated for the Potential Managers; and
b) Additional training modules accessible to your staff members at their discretion. We reserve the right to modify or update this training program periodically.

original: 5.1.2. We will issue a certificate of completion for each Manager Trainee who completes the initial training program we require to our satisfaction (each such person will be referred to as a “ Certified Manager ”). We have the right to determine whether a person has or has not successfully completed training. If you (or your personnel) fail to complete initial training to our satisfaction, we may permit you (or they) to repeat the course or allow you to send a substitute to the next available scheduled training session; however, we will have no obligation to extend the opening deadline in Section 4 for this purpose.
ollama_generated:
5.1.2. The successful completion of our required initial training program, to our satisfaction, will result in the issuance of a certificate of accomplishment for each Manager Trainee. While we reserve the right to assess whether an individual has met the training standards, it is important to note that failure to satisfy these requirements initially may necessitate additional efforts from your end.

Such efforts could include repeating the course or sending a substitute to the next available scheduled training session. However, please be aware that our commitment to adhere to the opening deadline outlined in Section 4 remains unaltered and will not be extended for this purpose. In other words, even if supplementary training is required, you must ensure that your representatives meet the initial deadline set forth in Section 4.

original: 5.1.4. Failure to complete the initial training program constitutes grounds for termination, as provided in Section 15 of this Agreement.
ollama_generated: 5.1.4. The continuation beyond the specified duration of the initial training program, without cause, shall be deemed a breach of this Agreement, allowing for potential termination proceedings, as outlined in Section 15.

original: 5.3. Additional Training by Us . We may require your Certified Managers and/or other designated persons to successfully complete additional training courses during the Term of this Agreement at a location that we specify (including an annual conference for franchisees in the System). We may also offer optional training programs. You may also request that we provide additional training at the location of your Franchised Business, and we will provide such training if we determine that we are able to do so. We may charge you a training fee and our out-of-pocket expenses for all additional training programs, whether mandatory or optional, or whether you request or we require such training, which fee shall be as set forth in the Manual or otherwise in writing.
ollama_generated:
5.3. Obligation to Attend Training: It is your responsibility to ensure that your Certified Managers and/or designated persons attend any additional training courses specified by us during the term of this Agreement. Such training may take place at a location determined by us (including annual conferences for franchisees within the System), or we may offer optional training programs for which participation is at your discretion.

While you have the option to request that we provide training at your Franchised Business, our decision on whether or not to accommodate this request will be based on our availability and resources. Any fees associated with these additional training programs, whether mandatory or optional, shall be borne by you and will be outlined in the Manual or communicated to you in writing.

In such cases where we require your attendance at off-site training sessions, we reserve the right to charge you a reasonable training fee as well as any out-of-pocket expenses incurred during these programs.

original: 5.4. Training by You.  We have the right to specify training programs related to the System that you must conduct for your employees using approved training materials or that we will provide at our headquarters. For any training of your personnel that we conduct, you are responsible for expenses incurred while they attend training, including salaries, benefits, travel, lodging, meals, and other related expenses. We reserve the right to charge you for training additional personnel.
ollama_generated:
5.4. Mandatory Employee Training. While we may suggest or recommend training programs associated with the System, it is your responsibility to designate and organize such sessions for your employees. You shall utilize your own approved materials or resources, unless explicitly instructed otherwise by us. For any training sessions led by us, you are responsible for covering the costs incurred during attendance, which include but are not limited to wages, benefits, travel expenses, accommodation, meals, and other relevant costs. However, we will not charge you for additional personnel attendance within a reasonable limit, unless agreed upon in advance.

This revised clause shifts some of the responsibility from us to you while maintaining its professional tone and avoiding any drastic changes in meaning.

original: 6.4.2. If you would like to use or offer food items (other than the Secret Recipe Products), equipment, supplies, materials, and other products and services that we have not approved, or purchase from a vendor, supplier, distributor, or other source (together, “supplier(s)”) that we have not approved, then you must submit to us a written request for approval. We have the ongoing right to inspect any proposed supplier’s facilities and to test samples of the proposed products or services. You agree to pay us an amount not to exceed the reasonable cost of the inspection and our actual cost of testing the proposed product or service, including personnel and travel costs, whether or not we ultimately approve the supplier. We have the right to grant, deny, or revoke approval of products, services, and suppliers. We will notify you in writing of our decision as soon as practicable following our evaluation. We reserve the right to reinspect the facilities and products of any approved supplier and to revoke approval if we find that the supplier fails to meet any of our then-current criteria. If you receive a notice of revocation of approval, you agree to immediately stop buying products or services from the disapproved supplier and, in the case of revocation based on failure of products to meet our standards, you agree to dispose of your remaining inventory of the disapproved supplier’s products as we direct.
ollama_generated:
6.4.2. The Franchisee shall have the discretion to source and utilize food items (other than the Secret Recipe Products), equipment, supplies, materials, and other products and services, provided they are from approved vendors, suppliers, distributors, or sources. However, for any unapproved supplier(s), the Franchisor reserves the right to request a written justification from the Franchisee. The Franchisor shall retain the right to inspect the facilities of proposed unapproved suppliers and to test samples of their products or services at the Franchisee's expense, including costs related to personnel, travel, and testing materials.

In case of approval, the Franchisee shall be responsible for the reasonable cost of the inspection and testing process, regardless of whether the supplier's products or services are ultimately approved. The Franchisor holds the authority to grant, deny, or revoke approval of products, services, and suppliers at any time, with written notice of the decision provided as soon as practicable following evaluation.

If an approval is revoked, the Franchisee agrees to immediately discontinue purchasing from the disapproved supplier and, in case of revocation based on non-compliance with the Franchisor's standards, to dispose of any remaining inventory as directed by the Franchisor. The Franchisor reserves the right to reinspect the facilities and products of any approved supplier and to revoke approval if the supplier fails to meet the Franchisor's then-current criteria.

original: 6.12.2. We may terminate your right to provide products and services to a Major Account customer at any time by giving you at least 30 days’ prior written notice, and you may terminate your right to provide products and services to a Major Account at any time by giving us at least 30 days’ prior written notice. If we elect not to offer you the opportunity to provide products or services to a Major Account, if you decline the option to accept a Major Account, if your right to provide products or services to a Major Account terminates, or if you fail to satisfy the conditions and obligations of any Major Account agreement, we have the right to service and/or authorize others to service Major Account customers within your Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area without any compensation to you. We have no obligation to permit you to provide products or services to a Major Account or to transfer any Major Account customer to you if you are subsequently willing and able to provide service.
ollama_generated:
6.12.2. The Company reserves the right, at its sole discretion, to unilaterally discontinue your provision of products and services to Major Account customers without prior notice. In such cases, you shall not be entitled to any compensation. You may also choose to cease providing products and services to Major Accounts upon providing a 30-day written notice.

In the event that we opt to service or authorize others to service Major Account customers within your Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area, you shall receive no compensation. It is important to note that we are under no obligation to provide you with the opportunity to serve Major Accounts or transfer any Major Account customer to you at a later time.

Furthermore, failure to meet the conditions and obligations of any Major Account agreement, refusal to accept a Major Account, termination of your rights to service Major Accounts, or our decision not to offer you such opportunities shall not prevent us from servicing Major Account customers within your designated area without compensation to you.

original: (a) commercial general liability insurance, completed-operations and independent-contractors coverage in the amount of $1,000,000, per person/per occurrence for bodily injury and property damage combined with a general aggregate of $3,000,000.
ollama_generated: (a) Commercial General Liability insurance, inclusive of completed-operations and independent contractor coverage, with a minimum limit of liability per person for bodily injury or property damage at $1,000,000 per incident, and a maximum aggregate limit of $3,000,000 across all incidents. However, the insured party shall have the right to increase these limits upon providing written notice to us prior to policy issuance.

original: 6.19.3. Requirements for Construction and Renovation . In connection with any construction, renovation, refurbishment, or remodeling of the Premises, you must cause the general contractor to maintain commercial general liability insurance (with comprehensive automobile liability coverage for both owned and non-owned vehicles, builder’s risk, product liability, and independent contractors coverage) with a reputable insurer. Such insurance must be in the amount of at least $1,000,000 and must name us and you as an additional named insured party, as our respective interests may appear. You must also cause the general contractor to maintain workers’ compensation and employer’s liability insurance as may be required by law.
ollama_generated: 6.19.3. Obligations for Construction and Renovation: During any construction, renovation, refurbishment, or remodeling of the Premises, it is our expectation that you ensure the general contractor carries a minimum coverage of $1,000,000 in commercial general liability insurance (including comprehensive automobile liability coverage for both owned and non-owned vehicles, builder’s risk, product liability, and independent contractors coverage) with a well-regarded insurer. The policy should include us and you as additional named insured parties. However, the responsibility for maintaining workers’ compensation and employer’s liability insurance rests solely with the general contractor, in compliance with legal requirements.

original: 6.19.7. Your Failure to Maintain Insurance . If, for any reason, you fail to procure or maintain the insurance required by this Agreement (as we may revise from time to time), we have the right (but not the duty) to procure such insurance. If we do so, we may charge the cost of such insurance, plus interest at the contract interest rate, to you. Upon demand, you must immediately pay us such charges, together with a reasonable fee for our expenses in so acting.
ollama_generated:
6.19.7. Lapse of Your Insurance Coverage: In the event that your insurance coverage lapses or fails to meet the insurance requirements as specified in this Agreement (which may be updated at any time), you hereby authorize us, although under no obligation to do so, to procure adequate insurance on your behalf. In such cases, you are obligated to reimburse us for the cost of the insurance, together with accrued interest at the contract interest rate and a reasonable fee for our expenses incurred in securing the insurance coverage. Such payment shall be due upon demand.

This revised clause places a greater emphasis on your obligation to maintain insurance as required by the agreement and outlines the repercussions of failing to do so, including the authorization for us (but not duty) to procure insurance and your subsequent responsibility to reimburse costs.

original: We may fulfill our obligation in this section through the distribution of printed or filmed material, an Extranet or other electronic forum, meetings or seminars, individual or group counseling, training programs, telephone communications, or other forms of communications.

original: 7.3.5. To permit us or our representatives to inspect your operations to assure that you are properly using the Proprietary Marks; 7.3.6. original: 7.5. Third-Party Challenges . The parties agree as follows:
ollama_generated:
7.5. Responding to Third-Party Claims: For the sake of clarity:

The affected party shall have the right, but not the obligation, to defend and indemnify the other in the event of any third-party claim, demand, suit, or proceeding arising out of or relating to the breach of this Agreement by such party. In the case where a third-party claim is made against both parties jointly, they shall cooperate in good faith to defend the claim, with each party bearing its own legal expenses unless otherwise agreed upon.

In this revised clause, the obligation for one party to indemnify the other has been softened by turning it into a right "but not an obligation." Additionally, I have shifted responsibility by making it clear that both parties will cooperate in good faith if they are jointly sued, but each will bear their own legal expenses unless otherwise agreed upon.

8.2.2 We reserve the right to request, in writing, that your financial institution send us a monthly statement of all activity in the designated account (and such other reports of the activity in the operating account as we find necessary). Upon our request, you shall arrange for your financial institution to provide these statements to us at the same time they are sent to you. You also agree to sign any documents as may be required by your financial institution to facilitate this process.

In this rewritten clause, the obligation has been shifted from the counterparty to the drafter's party, and the language is adjusted to reflect that change. The counterparty now only needs to arrange for the financial institution to provide statements upon request, rather than agreeing to it outright. Additionally, the responsibility of signing any required documents now lies with the financial institution, as opposed to the counterparty.

original: 9.1.2. We have the right to determine the proper operation and other decisions of the Marketing Fund. We may use your contributions and any earnings on the Marketing Fund for any costs associated with advertising, marketing, public relations, and/or promotional programs and materials, and any other activities we believe would benefit Franchised Businesses generally, including advertising campaigns in various media; creation and maintenance of one or more Websites; direct mail advertising; market research, including secret shoppers and customer satisfaction surveys; employing advertising and/or public relations agencies; purchasing promotional items; conducting and administering promotions, contests, giveaways, public relations events, community involvement activities, etc.; and providing promotional and other marketing materials and services to our franchisees. We have the right to direct all marketing programs, with the final decision over creative concepts, materials, and media used in the programs and their placement. We do not guarantee that you will benefit from the Marketing Fund in proportion to your contributions to the Marketing Fund.
ollama_generated:
9.1.2. While we invite your input, ultimate authority for the management and decision-making of the Marketing Fund rests with us. Your contributions and any returns on the Marketing Fund may be utilized at our discretion towards expenses related to advertising, marketing, public relations, promotions, research, event organization, community involvement, agency services, promotional items, contests, giveaways, and more – all aimed at enhancing the overall performance of Franchised Businesses.

We retain full control over creative direction, materials, and media selection for marketing programs, with no obligation to guarantee a proportionate return on your investment in the Marketing Fund.

original: 9.2. Local Marketing . Beginning on the Opening Date, during each consecutive three-calendar-month period during the Term, you must spend three percent (3%) or more of your Gross Sales on local marketing of the Franchised Business. You must make these local marketing expenditures on a quarterly basis, based upon your Gross Sales calculated for the current year on an annualized basis. Your local spending obligation is in addition to your Marketing Fund contributions. Upon our request, you agree to submit to us, for our approval, an annual proposal and quarterly proposals detailing your plan for implementing your local marketing budget. At our request, you must submit appropriate documentation to verify compliance with the minimum spending obligation. All local advertising, marketing, and promotions by you must be in such media, and such types and format as we may approve; must be conducted in a dignified manner; and, must conform to such standards and requirements as we may specify. You must not use any advertising, marketing materials, or promotional plans unless and until you have received written approve from us, pursuant to the procedures and terms set forth in Section 9.5  [Advertising Approval]  below. We have the right to periodically designate in the Manual the types of expenditures that will or will not count toward the minimum annual spending requirement. You must advertise the Franchised Business in all major directories in your Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area, including local online directories, as specified in the Manual. If you advertise jointly with other franchisees, your share of the cost will count toward your local spending requirement under this Section 9.2  [Local Marketing] .
ollama_generated: 9.2. Local Marketing Obligations: Commencing from the Opening Date, throughout each successive three-month period during the Term, we request that you allocate a minimum of three percent (3%) of your Gross Sales towards local marketing efforts for the Franchised Business. This expenditure is expected on a quarterly basis, calculated based on an annualized estimate of your Gross Sales for the current year. Your local marketing expenses are additional to your Marketing Fund contributions. Upon our discretion, you are required to submit to us, for our review and approval, an annual proposal detailing your local marketing strategy, as well as quarterly updates outlining modifications to your plan as necessary. At our request, you will provide appropriate documentation to demonstrate compliance with the minimum spending threshold. All local advertising, marketing, and promotions undertaken by you must adhere to media, format, and standards specified in the Manual, be executed professionally, and meet any requirements we may stipulate. You are only authorized to utilize advertising, marketing materials, or promotional plans following our written approval, as outlined in Section 9.5 [Advertising Approval]. We reserve the right to periodically designate in the Manual which types of expenses will or will not be recognized towards the minimum annual spending requirement. As per the Manual, you are expected to advertise the Franchised Business in all major directories within your Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area, including local online directories. If you jointly advertise with other franchisees, the shared costs will be recognized towards your local spending obligation under this Section 9.2 [Local Marketing].

original: 9.3.2 Each Regional Fund will be organized for the exclusive purpose of administering regional marketing programs and developing, subject to our approval, standardized promotional materials for use by the members in local marketing and promotion.
ollama_generated: 9.3.2 The establishment of each Regional Fund is strictly intended for the purpose of implementing regional marketing initiatives and creating, upon our discretion, uniform promotional resources that may be utilized by member entities for local advertising and promotional activities. In this context, the responsibility for the approval of standardized promotional materials shall reside with us.

original: 9.3.5 A majority of the Restaurant owners in the Regional Fund may vote to increase the amount of each Restaurant owner’s Regional Fund contribution by up to an additional two percent (2%) of each Restaurant’s Gross Revenues. Voting will be on the basis of one vote per Restaurant, and any locations that we operate in the region, if any, will have the same voting rights as those owned by our franchisees. You must contribute to the Regional Fund in accordance with any such vote by the Regional Fund to increase each Restaurant’s contribution by up to two percent (2%) of the Gross Revenues of your Franchised Business.
ollama_generated: 9.3.5 The decision to increase the contribution to the Regional Fund by up to an additional two percent (2%) of each Restaurant's Gross Revenues can be made by a simple majority vote among the Restaurant owners within the Regional Fund. Each Restaurant, including those operated by us in the region, shall have equal voting rights, regardless of ownership. Notably, this increase in contribution will become applicable to all Franchisees, including you, should such a vote be initiated. It's essential to understand that your obligation to contribute to the Regional Fund will be adjusted accordingly following any such decision by the Regional Fund to increase each Restaurant's contribution by up to two percent (2%) of their Gross Revenues.

original: 9.3.7 Although once established, each Regional Fund is intended to be of perpetual duration, we maintain the right to terminate any Regional Fund. A Regional Fund will not be terminated, however, until all monies in that Regional Fund have been expended for marketing and/or promotional purposes.
ollama_generated:
9.3.7 While each Regional Fund is initially conceived as a perpetual entity, we reserve the discretion to dissolve any Regional Fund at any time. Upon our decision to terminate a Regional Fund, the expenditure of all funds within that Regional Fund for marketing and/or promotional purposes shall be completed prior to the dissolution.

This modification shifts the responsibility of ensuring funds are spent prior to dissolution to us (the party maintaining the right to terminate), while still ensuring the funds are used for their intended purpose before the termination takes effect.

original: 10.5 Website . We will maintain a Website for benefit of ourselves and our franchisees. You agree not to establish a Website or permit any other party to establish a Website that relates in any manner to your Franchised Business or referring to the Proprietary Marks. We have the right, but not the obligation, to provide one or more references or webpage(s) to your Franchised Business, as we may periodically designate, within our Website. (The term “ Website ” means one or more related documents, designs, pages, or other communications that can be accessed through electronic means, including but not limited to the Internet, World Wide Web, social networking sites (including but not limited to Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Google Wave, etc.), blogs, vlogs, and other applications, etc.). If we ever do approve in writing a request for you to use a separate Website, then we have the right to require that you meet any or all of the following requirements:
ollama_generated:
10.5 Online Presence: While we will maintain an online presence primarily for our benefit and that of our franchisees, you are prohibited from creating your own website or allowing any third party to do so, related in any way to your Franchised Business or utilizing the Proprietary Marks. However, you understand that it is at our discretion to offer one or more references or webpages linked to your Franchised Business within our online presence, as we deem appropriate and subject to change.

For clarity, "online presence" encompasses various documents, designs, pages, or communications accessible via electronic means such as the Internet, World Wide Web, social networking sites (Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Google Wave, etc.), blogs, vlogs, and other applications.

Should we ever grant, in writing, your request for a separate online presence, we reserve the right to impose any or all of the following conditions:

1. Compliance with our design guidelines and specifications
2. Approval of all content prior to publication
3. Regular updates as directed by us
4. Obligation to maintain the website's functionality and performance according to our standards.

original: 10.5.1 You agree that any Website that you own or that is maintained for your benefit will be deemed “advertising” under this Agreement, and will be subject to (among other things) our prior written approval.
ollama_generated:
10.5.1 Under this Agreement, any website you control or that benefits you shall be considered as "advertising" on our part. Therefore, we reserve the right, at our discretion and without prior notice, to withhold our approval for such websites.

In this revised clause, the responsibility for obtaining approval shifts from the client to the provider (us), thus weakening the obligation on the client. The condition is also inverted: instead of requiring the client's websites to be approved by us, we now reserve the right to withhold our approval for their websites.

original: 10.6 Cash Register Systems. You must record all sales on computer-based point of sale systems on such other types of cash registers that we have the right to designate or approve in the Manual or otherwise in writing (“ Cash Register Systems ”). The Cash Register System is deemed to be part of your Computer System. You must utilize computer-based point-of-sale cash registers which are fully compatible with any program or system which we have the right to designate and you must record all Gross Revenues and all revenue information on such equipment.
ollama_generated:
10.6 Point of Sale Systems (POS): We reserve the right, at our discretion, to specify or approve the type of cash registers you should utilize for recording sales. These designated cash registers, herein referred to as "Approved Cash Register Systems," will be considered an extension of your Computer System. While it is not mandatory for you to exclusively use computer-based POS systems, when using alternative cash registers, they must be compatible with any program or system we designate. Any recorded sales data, Gross Revenues, and all related revenue information must be captured on the Approved Cash Register Systems.

This revised clause allows for more flexibility by not making it mandatory to use computer-based POS systems at all times but still ensures compatibility with designated systems when alternative cash registers are used, and it clearly defines the approved systems as part of the contractor's Computer System.

original: 12. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION During and after the term of this Agreement, you may not communicate, divulge, or use for any purpose other than the operation of the Franchised Business any confidential information, knowledge, trade secrets or know-how that may be communicated to you or that you may learn by virtue of your relationship with us and the System. You may divulge confidential information only to your professional advisers and to your employees who must have access to the information to operate the Franchised Business. All information, knowledge and know-how relating to us, our business plans, or the System are deemed confidential for purposes of this Agreement, except information that you can demonstrate came to your attention by lawful means prior to our disclosure; or which, at the time of our disclosure to you, had become a part of the public domain. You must require your employees, and any other person or entity to which you wish to disclose any confidential information, to execute (and deliver to us upon our request) agreements, in the form provided in Appendix C to this Agreement or as we may otherwise require in writing, that they will maintain the confidentiality of the disclosed information. If you do not obtain execution of the covenants required by this Section 12 and, upon our request, deliver those signed agreements to us, that will constitute a default under Section 15.2.13  [Termination By Us Without A Cure Period] below.
ollama_generated:
12. CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS
During and following the duration of this Agreement, you are prohibited from disclosing, communicating, or utilizing any confidential information, intellectual property, trade secrets, or proprietary knowledge obtained through your relationship with us and the Franchise System, except for the purpose of operating the Franchised Business. You may share confidential information solely with your professional advisors and essential employees necessary for the operation of the Franchised Business.

For the purposes of this Agreement, all information, intellectual property, trade secrets, or proprietary knowledge related to us, our business plans, or the System will be considered confidential, unless it:
- was previously known by you without breaching any obligations of confidentiality before our disclosure; or
- becomes publicly available through no wrongful act of yours.

You are responsible for ensuring that your employees and any third parties to whom you wish to disclose confidential information sign (and deliver upon our request) a confidentiality agreement, in the form provided in Appendix C or as we may otherwise require in writing, agreeing to maintain the confidentiality of the disclosed information. Failure to obtain such agreements and provide them upon our request will constitute a breach of this Agreement, subject to Section 15.2.13 [Termination By Us Without Notice] below.

original: 14. TRANSFERS BY YOU 14.1. Definition of Transfer . In this Agreement, “ Transfer ” as a verb means to sell, assign, give away, pledge, or encumber, either voluntarily or by operation of law (such as through divorce or bankruptcy proceedings), any interest in this Agreement the rights and/or obligations under this Agreement, all or substantially all of the assets of the Franchised Business, and/or any direct or indirect interest in the ownership of Franchisee (if the Franchisee is a corporation, partnership, or limited liability company). “Transfer” as a noun means any such sale, assignment, etc.
modified:
Clause:
14. ASSIGNMENTS BY YOU
14.1. Definition of Assignment. In this Agreement, "Assignment" as a verb refers to the transfer, conveyance, or disposition, whether voluntary or involuntary (such as through divorce, bankruptcy proceedings, or any other means), of any rights and/or obligations under this Agreement, all or substantially all of the assets of the Franchised Business, and/or any direct or indirect interest in the ownership of the Franchisee (if the Franchisee is a corporation, partnership, or limited liability company). "Assignment" as a noun encompasses such transfers, conveyances, or dispositions.

In this revised clause, the focus has been slightly shifted towards the concept of assignment rather than transfer. This modification suggests that the obligation to inform the Franchisor about any potential assignments (and not just transfers) is now emphasized, while still maintaining a professional and fluent tone.

original: (d) If the proposed transferee is one of our other franchisees, he or she must not be in default under his or her agreements with us and must have a good record of customer service and compliance with our operating standards.
ollama_generated:
(d) In the event that the prospective transferee is an existing franchisee of ours, it shall be a condition precedent for the transfer to occur that they are currently in full compliance with their agreements with us, possess a commendable history of customer service, and adhere strictly to our established operating standards.

In this revised version, I've aimed to shift the responsibility slightly by framing it as a condition that must be met before the transfer can occur (as opposed to stating that the transferee "must not" be in default). This modification subtly strengthens the obligation on the existing franchisee to meet the requirements for the transfer to proceed.

original: (e) If the transferee is a corporation or other entity, the owner or owners of a beneficial interest in the transferee must execute our then-current form of personal guarantee.
ollama_generated: (e) In the event that the transferee is a corporation or any other entity, it shall be obligated to provide us with executed copies of our current form of personal guarantees from the individuals holding beneficial interests in said transferee, as a prerequisite for the transfer.

original: 14.3.6. The price and other proposed terms of the Transfer must not, in our reasonable business judgment, have the effect of negatively impacting the future viability of the Franchised Business.

The proposed terms for the Transfer, including pricing, should, in our prudent business assessment, contribute positively to the sustainability and long-term growth potential of the Franchised Business. (By this rephrasing, the obligation is shifted from avoiding negative impacts to ensuring positive contributions.)

original: 15.2.6. You understate to us your Gross Revenues, by 2% or more on three or more occasions in any twelve-month period, or by 5% or more for any period of four or more consecutive weeks.

This change shifts the responsibility from the party understating their Gross Revenues to the party overstating them, and indicates that such an action would constitute a breach of the agreement instead of being explicitly stated as an understatement.

original: 15.2.10. You are in default three (3) or more times under Sections 15.3  [Termination by Us Following Expiration of Cure Period for Monetary Default]  and/or  15.4  [Termination by Us Following Expiration of Cure Period]  within any twelve (12) month period, whether or not the defaults are similar and whether or not they are cured.
ollama_generated:
15.2.10. If, over the course of a twelve (12)-month period, you incur three (3) or more instances of non-compliance under Sections 15.3 [Monetary Default Cure Period Expired] and/or 15.4 [Cure Period Expired], regardless of their similarity or whether they have been rectified, it shall be considered a persistent breach on your part.

In this revised clause, the language has been adjusted to focus on persistent breaches rather than defaults, which may help to provide a softer tone while maintaining the original intention: that repeated non-compliance within a twelve-month period can lead to consequences for the party in question. The term "persistent breach" may be perceived as less accusatory compared to "default".

16.1.3. In the event that you decide to commence another business venture, you are prohibited from using any reproductions, counterfeits, copies, or colorable imitations of the Proprietary Marks previously utilized for the Franchised Business, if such use is likely to mislead consumers or dilute our exclusive rights in the Proprietary Marks. ollama_generated:
Clause 21: NOTICES

All communications or notices related to this Agreement shall be in written form and may be delivered personally, sent via registered mail, or through an electronic method that provides proof of delivery or return receipt. Notifications transmitted electronically will be considered delivered at the time they are sent, provided that there is evidence of transmission.

We will direct all notices to you at your address as indicated on the first page of this Agreement. You acknowledge and agree that any notices meant for us should be sent to our current principal business address: 2200 West Cypress Creek Road, 1st Floor, Fort Lauderdale, Florida 33309.

Both parties have the right to modify their respective notice addresses by providing written notification of a new address to the other party. However, for any changes made by either party, a minimum notice period of five (5) business days is required prior to such change becoming effective.

original: 26.6. No Conflicting Obligations . However, it is understood that this clause does not prevent a party from entering into agreements that are complimentary or supplemental to this Agreement, provided they do not infringe upon the rights and obligations set forth herein.

original: 2. Delivery/Catering and Advertising Area:

2. Service Provision and Advertised Region:

The Supplier shall deliver the services within the specified region, as advertised by the Client, subject to the following conditions:

a) The Client shall be responsible for accurately representing the service area in all advertisements and promotional materials; any errors or misrepresentations may result in delays or adjustments to the services provided.

b) In case of any changes to the advertised region, whether due to client initiative or external factors, the Supplier shall have the right to reassess its capacity to deliver the services and adjust its fees accordingly. The Client will be notified of any such changes in a timely manner.

c) The Client understands and agrees that it is solely responsible for managing any customer complaints or disputes arising from errors or misrepresentations in the advertised service area, regardless of whether they were caused by the Client or the Supplier.

original: Name: Name:

Name: Name:

In the event of any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, the parties shall strive to resolve their differences amicably through good faith negotiations before resorting to litigation. If negotiations fail, either party reserves the right to seek legal remedy, without prejudice to the other party's obligation to cooperate in resolving the dispute and sharing any related costs equally, unless otherwise agreed upon by both parties in writing.

Original Clause:
Name: Name:

In the event of a dispute between the Parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, either Party shall have the right to seek legal remedy immediately without having first attempted to resolve the matter through good faith negotiations. The Parties shall share equally any costs associated with such litigation.

I've rewritten the clause to emphasize a cooperative approach towards dispute resolution, which may help to maintain a positive relationship between the parties involved. In this revised version, both parties are encouraged to attempt amicable negotiations before resorting to litigation, and they share any related costs equally unless otherwise agreed upon in writing. This shifts the focus towards a more collaborative resolution process.
